publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday June 1, 2024 at 03:25

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is organized into eight series: I. Correspondence, 1918-1949. II. Diaries, 1934-1936. III. Costa Rica, 1926 - 1949. IV. Photographs, 1918-1957. V. Printed material, 1915 - 1949. VI. Roosevelt campaign, 1930-1938. VII: World War I, 1917-1918. VIII. Writings, 1926-1948.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Leo R. Sack was born July 9, 1889. He attended the University of Missouri and later served during World War I in the United States Army Air Service.
From 1909 through 1933 Sack worked as a reporter and editor in Mississippi, Missouri, Texas, and Washington, D. C.

Sack served as Minister to Costa Rica from 1933 through 1937. Durning this time he also worked as a special assistant to James Farley, chairman of the Democratic National Committee for the 1936 presidential campaign. Sack resigned this post to join the Schenley Products Company in 1938 and in 1940 opened his own public relations firm in Washington, D. C.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Leo R. Sack Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, diaries, photographs, printed material, and other papers by, to, or relating to Leo R. Sack. It includes materials related to Sack's service in World War I, his career as a journalist and speechwriter for Franklin Delano Roosevelt, his work for the Franklin Delano Roosevelt's election campaign, and his involvement in the Zionist movement of the 1940s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains general correspondence. There is correspondence in other series as well, which was left in place to retain context.

did.unittitle: Correspondence, writings, and printed material related to the Palestine situation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters to Franklin D. Roosevelt from Leo R. Sack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains diaries created and kept by Leo R. Sack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains material relating to Leo. R. Sack's appointment as Minister to Costa Rica.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings about Leo R. Sack and Costa Rica.

did.unittitle: Translation of President Ricardo Jimenez speech given in reply to the speech of Leo R. Sack

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the translation of a speech by President Ricardo Jimenez, which was given in reply to a speech by Leo R. Sack upon the presentation of Sack's credentials as United States minister to Costa Rica on October 16, 1933.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thias series contains photographs signed and inscribed to Leo R. Sack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photograph with an illegible signature.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs with illegible signatures.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains clippings, pamphlets, and other printed ephemera related to Leo R. Sack's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Volume 135, no. 25 and two copies of Volume 137, no. 19.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies.

did.unittitle: American Zionist emergency council report of activities, 1940-1946

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings about politics, as well as Jewish people in America.

did.unittitle: Hawes, Harry: Freedom of the press

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains certificate for Washington chapter of the American Friends of the Hebrew University, along with related note found in printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains no. 1 (two copies), 2, 3 (two copies), 5 (two copies) 10 (two copies), 11, and 13.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains newspapers including Done Lunes, La Tribuna, The News, and Panama American.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Volume 2, nos.1-3 (two copies of no. 3).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains nos. 11-12 (two copies), no. 21, and nos. 25-26.

did.unittitle: Scripps-Howard News - Little dramas in the life of a great newspaper system
Certificate of appointment from Mississippi state governor
Supplemento Homenajae al cafe Costa Rica

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain clippings and pamphlets.

did.unittitle: Zionist Organization of America Balfour program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains materials relating to Leo R. Sack's work with Jim Farley, who ran Franklin Delano Roosevelt's campaign for president.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: History of Air Service Replacement Concentration Barracks No. 3, Base Section 1 American Expeditionary Forces
Vive la France et L a'ḿérique du Jaytausé Matetiere

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pen and ink sketch. by J. B. Capt. Jack.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains writings by Leo R. Sack.

did.unittitle: General Foulois, director of the flight to Berlin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies.
